audience.description: First-time and early-career developers, technicians, and operators who need a rigorous Linux command-line and operations foundation.
entryKnowledge: Use a keyboard and browser, create files, and distinguish local work from an internet service.
deviceConstraints: Modern browser; every terminal command runs only in a deterministic disposable simulator.
accessibilityAssumptions: All terminal output is selectable text, keyboard reachable, zoomable, and never dependent on color alone.

placementEvidence: Navigate files and explain a simple command only if a changed terminal task independently verifies the skill.
completionEvidence: Defend four cumulative Linux operations projects against changed state and safety constraints.
Pass the cumulative Linux performance exam at or above 85 percent.

includes: Filesystems, streams, text, identity, processes, packages, storage, services, networking, automation, security, and recovery
excludes: Kernel development and distribution packaging internals
Production orchestration, taught in Docker and Kubernetes courses

sources.title: The Open Group Base Specifications Issue 8
sources.scope: Controls portable shell, utility, process, file, environment, and system-interface semantics.

sources.title: GNU Bash Reference Manual
sources.scope: Controls Bash parsing, expansion, quoting, redirection, pipelines, startup, job control, scripting, and error behavior.

sources.title: GNU Coreutils Manual
sources.scope: Controls file, text, identity, process, filesystem, and common utility behavior.

sources.title: systemd Manual Pages
sources.scope: Controls unit, service, dependency, journal, boot, environment, and lifecycle behavior.

sources.title: Linux Kernel Userspace API Guide
sources.scope: Controls stable userspace interfaces and the boundary between command-line tools and kernel behavior.

competencies.statement: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.
misconceptions: The terminal window is the Linux shell and executes commands itself.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.

competencies.statement: Parse command names, options, operands, quoting, and exit status before predicting behavior.
misconceptions: Every token beginning with a hyphen is harmless formatting.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse command names, options, operands, quoting, and exit status before predicting behavior.

competencies.statement: Use man pages, help, type, command -V, info, and project documentation to establish command authority and provenance.
misconceptions: A search-result snippet is more authoritative than the installed manual.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use man pages, help, type, command -V, info, and project documentation to establish command authority and provenance.

competencies.statement: Capture current user, directory, platform, environment, and relevant state before making a bounded change.
misconceptions: If a command is familiar, a baseline observation is unnecessary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture current user, directory, platform, environment, and relevant state before making a bounded change.

competencies.statement: Record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.
misconceptions: A screenshot of output proves the conclusion without context.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.

competencies.statement: Derive default file and directory modes from requested modes and umask without treating umask as subtraction.

competencies.statement: Relate executable files, processes, threads, parentage, sessions, process groups, state, and open resources.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate executable files, processes, threads, parentage, sessions, process groups, state, and open resources.

competencies.statement: Use ps, pgrep, pidof, top-like evidence, and proc data with filters that identify the intended process uniquely.

competencies.statement: Use foreground, background, jobs, fg, bg, wait, disown, and nohup with terminal and session behavior in mind.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use foreground, background, jobs, fg, bg, wait, disown, and nohup with terminal and session behavior in mind.

competencies.statement: Interpret nice values, scheduling priority, resource limits, and pressure evidence without equating priority with guaranteed resources.
misconceptions: A negative nice value means a process uses fewer resources.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret nice values, scheduling priority, resource limits, and pressure evidence without equating priority with guaranteed resources.

competencies.statement: Distinguish shell variables, exported environment entries, positional parameters, and child-process inheritance.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish shell variables, exported environment entries, positional parameters, and child-process inheritance.

competencies.statement: Trace aliases, functions, builtins, hashed commands, PATH search, and explicit paths with type and command evidence.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace aliases, functions, builtins, hashed commands, PATH search, and explicit paths with type and command evidence.

competencies.statement: Control locale, character encoding, collation, numeric formatting, and timezone when reproducibility depends on them.

competencies.statement: Avoid exposing secrets through environment, history, process arguments, logs, or world-readable startup files.
misconceptions: Environment variables are a secret vault because child processes inherit them.

competencies.statement: Distinguish repositories, metadata, packages, dependencies, installed files, scripts, and transaction history.
misconceptions: A package manager merely downloads one executable file.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Query package origin, version, architecture, files, dependencies, reverse dependencies, and candidate changes before mutation.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate repository ownership, signing keys, transport, scope, pinning, and update policy before adding a source.

competencies.statement: Separate distribution packages, language/project environments, containers, and manually installed binaries by ownership and update path.
misconceptions: Installing every dependency system-wide makes projects more reproducible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate distribution packages, language/project environments, containers, and manually installed binaries by ownership and update path.

competencies.statement: Plan configuration preservation, cache use, downgrade constraints, service impact, and verification for package rollback.
misconceptions: Uninstalling a package always restores the machine to its previous state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan configuration preservation, cache use, downgrade constraints, service impact, and verification for package rollback.

competencies.statement: Relate block devices, partitions, filesystems, mount points, namespaces, and directory visibility.
misconceptions: A mounted device becomes a new drive letter independent of the directory tree.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate block devices, partitions, filesystems, mount points, namespaces, and directory visibility.

competencies.statement: Distinguish apparent size, allocated blocks, free space, inodes, deleted-open files, and per-directory usage.
misconceptions: A filesystem with free bytes cannot be full.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish apparent size, allocated blocks, free space, inodes, deleted-open files, and per-directory usage.

misconceptions: Device names such as /dev/sdb are permanent identities.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use lsblk, findmnt, mount, and filesystem identifiers to observe topology before any mount or format action.

competencies.statement: Create, inspect, extract, and verify archives while protecting path traversal, ownership, permissions, and compression expectations.
misconceptions: An archive and a compressed file provide the same structure and guarantees.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create, inspect, extract, and verify archives while protecting path traversal, ownership, permissions, and compression expectations.

competencies.statement: Define backup scope, consistency, retention, integrity, isolation, and restore testing as separate controls.
misconceptions: A successful backup command proves the data can be restored.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define backup scope, consistency, retention, integrity, isolation, and restore testing as separate controls.

competencies.statement: Relate service managers, units, dependencies, targets, process supervision, and application readiness.
misconceptions: An enabled service is necessarily running and healthy now.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate service managers, units, dependencies, targets, process supervision, and application readiness.

competencies.statement: Use status, is-active, is-enabled, show, list-dependencies, and failed-unit evidence before changing service state.

competencies.statement: Query journal fields, units, boots, priorities, time ranges, cursors, and follow mode without losing causal context.
misconceptions: journalctl always shows every application log regardless of configuration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Query journal fields, units, boots, priorities, time ranges, cursors, and follow mode without losing causal context.

competencies.statement: Inspect vendor units, drop-ins, environment sources, daemon reload requirements, and effective configuration.
misconceptions: Editing a vendor unit in place is the safest persistent customization.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect vendor units, drop-ins, environment sources, daemon reload requirements, and effective configuration.

competencies.statement: Define restart limits, readiness checks, dependency order, failure escalation, and rollback evidence for a service change.
misconceptions: Automatic infinite restart is a complete availability strategy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define restart limits, readiness checks, dependency order, failure escalation, and rollback evidence for a service change.

competencies.statement: Interpret interface addresses, routes, neighbors, and listening or established sockets as different network evidence.
misconceptions: A configured IP address proves the application port is reachable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret interface addresses, routes, neighbors, and listening or established sockets as different network evidence.

competencies.statement: Trace hosts files, resolver configuration, search domains, DNS queries, caches, and application lookup results.
misconceptions: Successful ping proves DNS and the application are both healthy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace hosts files, resolver configuration, search domains, DNS queries, caches, and application lookup results.

competencies.statement: Test local process, local socket, route, name resolution, transport, and application protocol with the smallest useful probe.
misconceptions: Repeated broad scanning is more informative than layered tests.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test local process, local socket, route, name resolution, transport, and application protocol with the smallest useful probe.

competencies.statement: Separate server host identity, user authentication, authorization, agent forwarding, and known-host decisions.
misconceptions: Accepting a changed SSH host key is safe when the username and password still work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate server host identity, user authentication, authorization, agent forwarding, and known-host decisions.

competencies.statement: Use scp, sftp, rsync, and SSH forwarding with explicit direction, permissions, resume, exposure, and verification.
misconceptions: SSH port forwarding exposes only harmless local traffic by definition.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use scp, sftp, rsync, and SSH forwarding with explicit direction, permissions, resume, exposure, and verification.

competencies.statement: Define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation.
misconceptions: A script is correct if it succeeds once on the author’s machine.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation.

competencies.statement: Use set options, traps, explicit conditionals, and status handling while understanding where errexit and pipefail do not behave intuitively.
misconceptions: set -e converts any Bash script into fail-safe automation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use set options, traps, explicit conditionals, and status handling while understanding where errexit and pipefail do not behave intuitively.

competencies.statement: Handle positional parameters, defaults, validation, arrays, and quoted expansion without word splitting or glob injection.
misconceptions: Using $@ without quotes preserves argument boundaries.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle positional parameters, defaults, validation, arrays, and quoted expansion without word splitting or glob injection.

competencies.statement: Create temporary resources safely and guarantee cleanup across success, expected failure, signals, and partial work.
misconceptions: A fixed /tmp filename is safe when a script runs quickly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create temporary resources safely and guarantee cleanup across success, expected failure, signals, and partial work.

competencies.statement: Test scripts with fixtures, changed inputs, failure injection, shell analysis, exit status, and idempotence checks.
misconceptions: A syntax check exercises command behavior and cleanup.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test scripts with fixtures, changed inputs, failure injection, shell analysis, exit status, and idempotence checks.

competencies.statement: Form competing hypotheses, select discriminating observations, and update confidence without changing multiple layers at once.
misconceptions: Trying plausible fixes quickly is equivalent to diagnosis.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Form competing hypotheses, select discriminating observations, and update confidence without changing multiple layers at once.

competencies.statement: Use privilege only for the smallest justified operation while preserving command provenance, environment boundaries, and audit evidence.
misconceptions: Prefixing an entire workflow with sudo is safer than handling permissions deliberately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use privilege only for the smallest justified operation while preserving command provenance, environment boundaries, and audit evidence.

competencies.statement: Preview scope, snapshot relevant state, define stop conditions, and test rollback before risky filesystem, package, or service changes.
misconceptions: A rollback plan can be invented after a failed change.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preview scope, snapshot relevant state, define stop conditions, and test rollback before risky filesystem, package, or service changes.

competencies.statement: Build a timeline from clocks, journals, command history, process state, configuration changes, and observed symptoms.
misconceptions: Log order always equals causal order across services and clocks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build a timeline from clocks, journals, command history, process state, configuration changes, and observed symptoms.

competencies.statement: Write an accessible runbook with prerequisites, exact observations, safe actions, expected signals, exceptions, recovery, and ownership.
misconceptions: A list of commands is a sufficient operations handoff.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write an accessible runbook with prerequisites, exact observations, safe actions, expected signals, exceptions, recovery, and ownership.
